isolation.mysql: mreviewer on 127.0.0.1:3306 via docker exec mreviewer-mysql

assertions.title: MR open event creates review run
assertions.reason: The open webhook returned 200 accepted and persisted both a hook_events row and a pending review_runs row for MR 7301.

assertions.title: MR update with new commit creates review run
assertions.reason: The update webhook returned 200 accepted and added a second pending review_run for the new head SHA on the same MR.

assertions.title: MR close/merge event terminates active runs
assertions.reason: Both close and merge webhooks returned 200 accepted and flipped one seeded running row plus one pending row for their MR to cancelled while updating merge_requests.state to closed/merged.

assertions.title: MR close cancels pending run
assertions.reason: After the close webhook, the pending run for MR 7302 had status cancelled, non-cancelled run count stayed at 0 after a 3-second wait, and comment_actions remained empty.

frictions.description: The ingress binary on port 3100 does not run the scheduler, so HTTP-only traffic leaves runs in pending; to exercise VAL-INGRESS-005 against a running row, a targeted SQL update was required for the isolated test MR before sending close/merge webhooks.
frictions.resolution: Updated exactly one run per isolated MR to status=running via docker exec mysql, then verified the subsequent close/merge webhook cancelled both the seeded running row and the remaining pending row.
